steps.title: Identify what the underlined portion does
steps.content: The underlined phrase — "thus capitalizing on the research potential offered by a vast but hitherto relatively underutilized source of insight" — uses the word capitalizing on and evaluates the source as "vast but hitherto relatively underutilized." This language judges the value and significance of using historical DNA, rather than specifying applications, highlighting findings, or explaining why the method is rare.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It specifies potential applications of the approach that Kessler and colleagues used in their study.: the underlined portion makes no mention of specific applications (e.g., which organisms to study next). It evaluates the source's potential, not its concrete uses. ✗
  • Choice B — It emphasizes the importance of Kessler and colleagues' findings about the DNA of pathogens.: the underlined portion is about the approach (using historical DNA), not the findings or results of the study. ✗
  • Choice C — It offers commentary on the significance of the approach that Kessler and colleagues used for their study.: "capitalizing on the research potential offered by a vast but hitherto relatively underutilized source" is evaluative commentary about how important and advantageous this methodological choice is. ✓
  • Choice D — It explains why the research methodology selected by Kessler and colleagues is not widely used.: the phrase notes the source is underutilized but does not explain why — no causal reason is given. ✗
That matches Choice C — It offers commentary on the significance of the approach that Kessler and colleagues used for their study..
